He feared that his employer’s other identity would be found out. 
Corbin was engrossed in his thoughts and didn’t see the automobile ahead. So he braked hard…
###Chapter 144
Eliana’s body moved forward uncontrollably because of the sudden impact. 
She was so terrified that her heart almost leaped out of her chest. 
A pair of warm hands wrapped around her waist and pulled her back. 
When Eliana came back to her senses, she was already in Maurice’s arms. He was holding her very tightly. 
“Are you okay?” 
Maurice’s low, hoarse voice came from above her head. Eliana’s face immediately turned red, and she jerked away from his arms. 
“Ye…yeah” She shook her head, trying to calm her racing heart. 
Corbin turned around and apologized, “I’m sorry, boss. It was my fault,” 
“Be careful,” said Maurice, straightening his tie. 
The car was restarted. The embarrassing stalemate was immediately broken after the minor mishap. The atmosphere between Eliana and Maurice became intimate again. 
“You have yet to respond to my question.” Eliana dared not to look directly into Maurice’s eyes. 
Maurice took the phone and pretended to examine the photograph. “I know this ring, and I’m actually looking for it,” he said calmly. 
“Is it yours?” Eliana asked. 
“It’s not mine. I just want to collect it.”
That was it? 
Eliana still did not believe Maurice. 
Would Hodge make such an effort to find something so insignificant? He had even lured her with the truth in the past. 
“Why are you so interested in this ring? Do you know where it is?” 
Maurice stared at Eliana. His deep eyes seemed to be able to see right into her soul. 
Eliana was agitated. “Of course not. How could I know something you don’t even know?” 
Maurice looked so calm. He didn’t appear to be lying. 
Eliana had to temporarily put her doubts aside. 
Besides, it was easy to get information whenever necessary. 
Eliana breathed a sigh of relief. She then realized something crucial. 
Preston hadn’t lied to her because the ring was indeed not Maurice’s. 
Damn it! She had wronged the gigolo again. 
She wished she could just vanish when she remembered the messages she had sent. 
Eliana was the type of person who couldn’t keep her emotions to herself. Her face could clearly show what she felt. Maurice noticed her face changing in a matter of seconds, from being relaxed to complacent to regretful. 
Maurice was amused by the quick change in her lovely expressions.
After a few moments, Eliana asked to get out of the car because the kindergarten was already nearby. 
“I have arrived at my destination. Thank you.” 
Eliana got off the car. Maurice watched her walk away thoughtfully. 
“Boss, it seems that Eliana is also heading to the kindergarten. Is she picking up the kids for Nana?” Corbin guessed. 
Kids? 
Images of the twins flashed immediately in Maurice’s mind. 
The little girl was lovely and cute, and the little boy was cool and handsome as well. He had no idea why the images of the twins overshadowed Eliana’s face in his mind. 
Maurice thought the twins resembled Eliana more than Nana. 
Something flashed through Maurice’s mind out of nowhere. 
“Are we still picking up Silas?” 
Corbin’s voice broke into Maurice’s thoughts. 
“No, have the bodyguards take him out first. Don’t let Eliana see you there,” Maurice ordered. 
He didn’t want to reveal that Silas was his nephew just yet. 
Meanwhile, Eliana thought that she had done a good job keeping the secret as she took a detour to pick up the children at the kindergarten gate.
It was the end of school hours. As time passed, the adults who had been waiting with her had already left with the other children they picked up, but Aileen and Adrian were nowhere to be found. 
Eliana became uneasy. Was there something wrong? 
All of a sudden, she saw the teacher of the twins. 
The usually amiable teacher had a straight face and asked, “You are Aileen and Adrian’s mother, aren’t you?” 
Something must have happened. 
“Yes. What happened to the kids?” Eliana asked anxiously. 
“Nothing serious. It was just a minor squabble between the kids. Come with me,” the teacher said seriously. “Aileen broke another child’s valuable item.” 
Aileen? 
Eliana was taken aback when she heard that. How could her obedient daughter make trouble?
###Chapter 145
Eliana followed the teacher into the classroom. 
Both adults and children were present. Two groups of people were facing off in the center of the room. 
Aileen and Adrian were standing on one side. Aileen’s eyes were red from crying and tears streaked down her cheeks. 
Adrian stood on her right and comforted her in a low voice. Silas stood on her left and wiped her tears. Behind them stood Silas’s bodyguards. 
Eliana also recognized the other group of people. 
Erica was in the lead. 
Why was this woman always trying to make her life harder? 
Eliana paid no attention to Erica as she strode over to Aileen and squatted in front of her. She caressed Aileen’s tiny face and looked at her gently. 
“What happened, my love? Don’t cry. Mommy is here. Tell me what happened.” 
Her mother’s comfort only made Aileen felt more aggrieved. She pursed her lips and stayed silent. Adrian was about to explain the situation, but a sharp voice interrupted him. 
“Eliana, you need to discipline your daughter. She is a little liar! She refuses to admit that she broke my daughter’s diamond necklace. She is so ill-bred. Well, it’s not surprising, since her father is a…” 
“Shut up, Erica! Watch your mouth, or you’ll regret it!” Eliana shouted. 
Everyone was stunned by Eliana’s outburst. Everyone except Erica, who was unfazed.
“Eliana, don’t think that you can scare me just because of what happened with the pink diamonds last time. You are far below my league.” 
As she spoke, Erica held up the necklace, which was broken into two pieces. 
“Everyone here can judge who is right and who is wrong,” she said to the crowd. “I bought this precious diamond necklace as a gift for my daughter. My daughter showed it to your daughter. Your daughter was jealous and broke it. Now she refuses to admit what she has done. What a cheap little liar! You won’t be getting away with this.” 
The crowd whispered to each other and exchanged glances. Erica was satisfied to see that most of them were on her side. 
Now that the Harrison family had gone through a crisis, her relationship with Asher was as good as before. Her status was far above that of Eliana, whose family had already fallen. Eliana was no competition to her. 
“I didn’t do it!” Aileen shouted. “Mommy, I didn’t break the necklace. It was broken when Ivy showed it to me.” 
Aileen gripped Eliana’s hand. Her face turned red and all of her little body trembled in anger. 
“That is true!” Adrian affirmed. 
Eliana’s heart ached. She hugged Aileen and patted her back. “Baby, it’s okay. I believe you.” 
Aileen slowly calmed down. 
Once Aileen had been comforted, Eliana walked to Erica’s daughter, Ivy. 
She squatted down and asked gently, “Ivy, can you tell me what happened?”
Ivy stared timidly into Eliana’s eyes. She didn’t dare to speak. 
Ivy liked Eliana very much because she was gentler than her own mother. 
The truth was that Ivy had broken the necklace. She was terrified that her mother would punish her for it. She asked her friend Aileen for advice and showed her the necklace. It was at that moment that Erica entered. Erica insisted that Aileen had broken the necklace. 
Ivy opened her mouth to speak. But her mother shot her a deadly glance that scared Ivy into hiding the truth. 
“The necklace… Aileen broke the necklace.” 
Though she spoke quietly, everyone heard her. 
Erica smiled in triumph. “Did you hear that? Your daughter broke the necklace. It is your responsibility to compensate me for it.” 
Eliana raised her eyebrows. “If you want me to compensate for this necklace, I need to take a look at it so I can judge the price.” 
Erica thought that she was already the victor of this game, so she thoughtlessly handed over the necklace. 
Eliana accepted the necklace and examined it carefully. It was an ordinary diamond necklace with a basic design. The diamond seemed to be expensive at first glance, as it looked like it weighed about 6 carats. The rest of the necklace was made of cheap pearls. 
There was something off about the diamond. 
Wait a moment.
Eliana realized what was wrong. 
Eliana turned to the crowd and said confidently, “This diamond is a fake.” 
Erica’s smile froze. “That’s impossible. You’re only claiming that because you don’t have the money to compensate for it!” 
Eliana smiled in disdain at Erica’s refusal to admit the fraud. 
She didn’t have the time or energy to argue with Erica. She put the necklace on the table, picked up a glass, and smashed it into the diamond. 
“What are you doing? Are you crazy?” Erica yelled.
###Chapter 146
Eliana struck the diamond with the glass, and the fake diamond shattered into pieces with a loud bang. 
Shocked, everyone looked at Erica. 
“Looks like you gave your daughter a fake diamond, and tried to blackmail me. I should let the police handle the matter.” 
With a meaningful smile, Eliana picked up the broken necklace. 
“Oh my God! Did she really buy a fake?” 
“She was even forcing Aileen’s mother to pay for it. Maybe, she was looking to extort money from the later!” 
“She could have broken the necklace herself, you know? How could she ask her own child to lie for her? That’s a sin.” 
Everyone present began to sneer at Erica with a disgusted look in their eyes. 
She felt really embarrassed. 
Eliana then proposed to call the police. 
Watching her take out her phone and proceed to dial a number, Erica became flustered and shouted, “Stop her! Take the necklace back.” 
In just a second, her bodyguards rushed over. 
Silas’ bodyguards had frightened her before, so now, she brought her bodyguards with her every time she came to the kindergarten to pick up her child.
Silas ordered in a childish voice, “Don’t let her succeed!” 
The next second, his bodyguards rushed to Erica’s bodyguards and began to push them away. 
Seeing that, Erica snatched the necklace and ran away with Ivy. 
Eliana became furious. 
‘How dare she try to escape?’ She decided to catch her and teach her a lesson. 
Just when she was about to chase Erica, she felt a small hand grabbing her dress. 
Eliana looked down at Aileen, who seemed to be terrified. 
“Mommy…” 
It was only natural for a kid to be afraid in such a chaotic situation. 
Glancing at the kids, Eliana figured that it was more important to watch over them than to chase after Erica. 
She helped Aileen sit down in a chair and stroked her hair lovingly. 
“It’s okay, baby. I’m right here. No one will dare to bully you while I am here, okay? Look, even Adrian and Silas are with you.” 
Sniffling, Aileen looked up and saw Adrian and Silas standing before her with a concerned look in their eyes. 
Although she felt a little relieved, she was still heartbroken by her friend’s betrayal. 
“Mommy, why did Ivy lie? Wasn’t she my friend?”
Aileen looked at Eliana pitifully with tears in her eyes. 
Eliana sighed and asked, “Aileen, why do you think Ivy didn’t dare to tell the truth?” 
“Is it because her mother didn’t allow her to?” 
Aileen became afraid at the thought of Erica, and reminded herself that Eliana was the best mother in the world. 
With a warm smile, Eliana said, “You are so smart, Darling! Tell me, would you ever lie if I asked you to?” 
Puzzled by her mother’s question, Aileen thought for a while before she shook her head. 
“No, I won’t.” 
“That’s right. Children should not do everything that adults ask them to. They should learn to distinguish right from wrong on their own. I hope that you turn out to be a sensible and honest woman in the future, Kiddo. You did great today!” 
Hearing that, Adrian complained, “Mommy, you are so corny! You’ve said that so many times already.” 
Eliana frowned, feeling speechless. She did not expect that her son would not agree with her. 
And she really did not know what to do with him. 
She quickly wiped away Aileen’s tears. “I know that I said it a lot, but it works.” 
Upon hearing those comforting words, Aileen felt better. 
“Aileen, here you go.”
Silas handed her the candy that his teacher had given him earlier. “A kid who lies is not a good person. Don’t talk to Ivy anymore.” 
Aileen had always consoled him by giving him a piece of candy and saying sweet words to him whenever he had been upset. 
Taking the candy from him, Aileen gave him a firm nod. 
She also did not want to be friends with a girl who lied to her. 
As she saw the kids getting along with each other, Eliana’s heart warmed up. 
“Mr. Gorman, it’s time for you to go home,” a bodyguard urged Silas. 
“Let’s go together.” 
Eliana touched Silas’ head, thinking that she should thank his bodyguards. 
“Okay.” 
Eliana and the kids walked out of the school, followed by the bodyguards. At the same time, Maurice’s car was parked not too far away from the school gate. 
“Oh, no! Mr. Moran, Mr. Gorman is walking out of the school with Miss Pierce and the twins!” 
The moment Corbin saw them, he immediately turned around and informed Maurice. 
Why were they coming out together? 
Frowning, Maurice could not come up with any plans, so he said, “Try to avoid them, then.” 
As the CEO of the Moran Group, he never thought that he would be hiding from people.
Silas saw Maurice’s car and was about to wave to him. However, the car drove away at a high speed. 
Silas was puzzled. ‘What happened? I didn’t even get in the car!’
###Chapter 147
Did he really see what he thought he saw? 
Silas felt something was weird. He immediately called Maurice on his smart watch. 
“Hello, uncle. Why aren’t you here to pick me up yet?” 
“I’m on my way. Wait a little.” 
Maurice asked Corbin to park the car on a nearby corner. From there they could secretly observe Eliana and the children. 
“You are always busy with work. Are you still working? Have you forgotten what day it is?” 
Silas was irritated and hurt. 
Before Maurice could explain, Silas had already hung up in disappointment. 
Silas stood still in silence. His little body drooped down, making him look like an abandoned puppy. 
Aileen and Adrian glanced at each other. “Silas, if your uncle isn’t picking you up, you can come to our house,” Aileen said. “Bowling isn’t there, but we can play with Foodie.” 
Adrian bumped Silas’ shoulder. “That’s right. Let’s go to our house and play.” 
“Not today.” Silas shook his head and looked down at the ground. He was silent. 
Eliana could tell that something was wrong with Silas. 
He was usually a polite and sensible kid, and it was strange for him to reject an invitation and ignore his friends. 
Eliana squatted down, grasped his hand, and asked gently, “Silas, can you tell me why you are sad?”
Eliana’s kindness reached Silas’ heart. He bit his lip and tears welled up in his eyes. “Today is the day… my parents died. But my uncle has forgotten about it.” 
This stunned Eliana. She could see her younger self in Silas. She knew better than anyone the pain of losing parents. 
Poor kid… 
Eliana hugged and comforted Silas. “Silas, you have to believe that your uncle has not forgotten what day it is today. You lost your parents, and your uncle also lost his sister. How could anyone who lost their loved one not feel grief? He must have something going on today. I’ll stay with you and wait for a while, okay?” 
As a mother of two, Eliana knew that childcare was not easy. 
After Silas’ parents died, only his uncle was left to take care of him. Since he was also working, it was inevitable that he would not be able to give Silas his complete attention. 
After a while, a Lincoln limousine came to a stop in front of Eliana. She could barely see a man wearing sunglasses and a mask in the driver’s seat. 
The windows into the back seat were completely dark. 
The driver looked strangely familiar. 
As Eliana tried to get a closer look at him, he said, “Mr. Gorman, I apologize for my tardiness. Please step in the car.” Corbin tried to make his voice sound different. 
Eliana frowned when she heard the driver’s strange voice. 
Silas was also confused, but he didn’t want to. He said goodbye to Eliana and the twins.
“Miss Pierce, Aileen, Adrian, I’m leaving now. Goodbye.” 
“Have a nice dinner with your uncle.” Eliana hugged Silas. 
Silas waved goodbye to the three of them and got into the limousine. 
“Where is Maurice?” Silas asked Corbin suspiciously. 
“Here.” Maurice sat up in the back seat. 
Maurice was late because he had changed to a different car to avoid Eliana recognizing him. 
“Silas, I’m sorry. I haven’t forgotten what today is. We agreed to have dinner together today, and I won’t break my promise.” Maurice’s voice was sincere. 
Silas was surprised and turned around. The kind man in front of him was completely different from his indifferent uncle. 
Silas remembered what Eliana said. He couldn’t help but ask, “Uncle, when my mom died… were you sad?”
###Chapter 148
Maurice was so stunned that his mind went blank for a moment. 
Sad? 
“Silas, how could I not be sad?” Pain shone in Maurice’s eyes. “Your mother was my only sister,” he said slowly. “When I lost her, it felt like my world was collapsing. But I still have you, Rosina’s only child. I have sworn to protect you as well as I can. Do you understand?” 
Memories filled Maurice’s mind, accompanied by a sharp pain in his heart and a sadness so strong he felt like he couldn’t breathe. 
Before their parents died, they told them to support each other and told Maurice to protect his sister, but… 
He had lost Rosina forever… 
To others, Maurice seemed crazy after Rosina’s death. All he could do was distract himself with endless work. 
If he hadn’t have Silas to look after, he didn’t know if he could have held on. 
Silas watched Maurice silently. Though they lived together, they had never talked with each other so candidly before. 
Silas used to think that Maurice was responsible for his parents’ death. If they hadn’t taken Maurice’s car, they would have still been alive. When Maurice immersed himself in his work, Silas thought that meant he wasn’t sad about their death. Now, for the first time, he understood the grief Maurice was feeling. 
Eliana had been right. His uncle was as grief-stricken as he was.
“D-don’t be too upset. Miss Pierce told me that you wouldn’t forget what today is. I shouldn’t have blamed you,” Silas said, giving Maurice an awkward pat on the shoulder. 
‘Miss Pierce? That’s Eliana.’ 
Maurice felt a wave of warmth melting the ice around his heart. 
Silas thought that Aileen had been wronged today. He complained angrily to Maurice, “Miss Pierce is so kind, but someone is always being mean to her. Ivy Harrison’s mother was bullying Aileen and Miss Pierce today. She used a fake diamond necklace to frame Aileen. She said that Aileen had broken it and demanded that Miss Pierce compensated for it. She is so hateful!” 
Silas told Maurice about the situation in a clear manner. 
Ivy Harrison? She was Erica’s daughter. So Erica was causing trouble for Eliana again? 
Maurice guessed the truth immediately. 
“Don’t worry. Bullies will come to a bad end.” 
Maurice touched Silas’ head and decided that he would teach Erica a lesson in the future. 
The limousine sped along and soon arrived at the old mansion. 
Maurice got out and opened the door for Silas. 
As usual, he reached out his hand to grasp Silas’. 
Silas stiffened for a moment, but he didn’t refuse. 
Maurice led Silas into the old mansion.
Earle Moran, the real leader of the Moran family, was sitting on the sofa in the living room. 
He was a legend in the business world for decades. He was now in his seventies. 
His hair and beard were all white, and he wore a black suit. His face was hard and cold. A pair of domineering eyes flashed with an icy light from within deep sockets. Even sitting on the sofa in his home, he seemed alienated from others. 
Maurice had that same domineering aura. But Earle’s aura grew stronger as time went by, until just a glance from him could make one’s blood run cold. 
“Why do you return so late?” Earle’s deep voice sounded like it had the power to penetrate into the depths of a listener’s heart. 
Silas couldn’t stop himself from cowering behind Maurice. 
“Maurice, today is an important day, and it is inappropriate for you to be late.” Gerald seized the opportunity to echo Earle. 
Maurice was used to it. 
Even on a day like this, his uncle would not miss an opportunity to belittle him in front of his grandfather.
###Chapter 149
“You’re both here, Maurice and Silas.” 
In the blink of an eye, the chilly feeling in the room dissipated when Nancy walked into the living room with a dish of fruit. 
“What are you referring to?” 
“It’s nothing, Mom,” Gerald said. He had to treat his mother with respect. 
Nancy snarled at Earle and Gerald. “What’s the matter with you? Surely you don’t want to frighten the kid.” 
She took Silas in her arms. 
Obediently, Silas leaned towards Nancy’s breast. When he wasn’t acting like an adult and acting like a child, he was lovable. 
“Dear Great-grandma, how are you?” 
“You poor little thing, the last time I saw you was long ago. I miss you terribly.” 
Nancy gave Silas a pinch on the face and a kiss on the cheek. 
Silas gathered his strength to speak, raising his head. “Don’t blame Uncle Maurice, Great-grandpa. Because I had been playing for a long time, we arrived late.” 
When Earle saw his granddaughter’s sole child, his expression softened. He gave Maurice an uninterested look and said, “Do not repeat that. Sit down. I guess it’s only right if we have a meal.” 
“Sure.”
Immediately after Earle’s comments, everyone sat down. There was silence for a minute, except for the sound of plates and forks hitting. 
Gerald was upset when he saw how peaceful the room was. 
He feigned concern and said, “Maurice, I’ve heard that you tore up the Jarvis household in order to win the affections of a lady. Is this accurate?” 
Inwardly, Maurice scoffed as he put down the food he was picking up. 
Today, Gerald tried to make it as tough as possible for him. 
Earle’s scowl was anticipated, and he enquired, “Maurice, tell me what’s happening. Who’s that woman?” 
Maurice laid down his forks, wiped his mouth gently, and answered, “Grandpa, I didn’t wipe out the Jarvis family for the sake of a woman. In recent years, the Jarvis family has been a spent arrow. They still hoped to gain an edge by using the closeness of the Moran family to them. Allow them to take advantage of us, and more families will follow suit and do the same in the future. I can’t let that happen!” 
After he heard this, Earle’s expression became a little softer. 
Gerald was rather persistent. “Really? Everyone in the workplace was aware that you pursued Eliana, according to what I’ve heard.” 
Earle squinted and asked, “Eliana?” 
Gerald’s responded, “Yes, that’s correct. She is the sole daughter of the Pierce Group’s owner, and the group declared bankruptcy five years ago.”
“I advise you, Maurice, although you may have a good time with a low-status lady, you should not take things to a serious level. The tragedy of the last generation is something that shouldn’t recur. Especially if you’ll be one involved.” 
Maurice’s father’s demise saddened Earle. Every time he mentioned him, he was overcome with grief. 
These statements were unbearable to Nancy. She chastised Earle by pointing her finger at his nose. “All of you, please stop talking now. Earle, I’d want to tell you something. I’m happy to have Eliana as my granddaughter-in-law. Come on, someone, stop it! Didn’t everyone learn enough from the past? I will flee if somebody tries to cause problems for Maurice again. Leaving the Moran family is an option for me!” 
Earle remained silent as a result of the scolding. This dominant husband, who had been that way his whole life, couldn’t stand up to his wife. His wife appeared to be able to persuade him to give in to her. 
Gerald was unable to say anything harsh when Nancy became enraged. 
They were all awestruck by Nancy. 
Silas gave Nancy a sneaky thumbs-up beneath the table. 
Nancy was able to settle a disagreement right away. 
Maurice rarely sat in front of Silas’ bed in the evenings to lull him to sleep. On the nightstand, there was a snapshot of the whole family. 
“Sleep now. I’m not going anywhere till you’re sound asleep.” 
Silas’s quilt was tugged by Maurice, who perched on the side of the bed. 
“Then you should keep your word,” Silas whispered, burying his head in the comforter.
“Of course.” Maurice’s grin became softer and softer around the corners of his lips. 
Maurice didn’t leave until Silas was well asleep. 
It was already far into the wee hours of the morning. Maurice went alone to the balcony and started smoking. He could only really experience his yearning for someone at that exact time.
###Chapter 150
Maurice’s alone time was soon interrupted by an unwelcomed guest. 
It was Gerald. 
“Maurice, it is such a surprise to see that you dote on your nephew so much, even though you’re not even married yet. It’s such a pity that your sister died at such a young age.” 
The cigarette between Maurice’s fingers slipped to the floor and he turned around with an unmistakable fierceness in his eyes. 
He grabbed Gerald’s neck and pressed him against the balcony railing, the sheer force causing half of his body to hang in the air as though he was about to fall to his death any moment. 
Although Gerald was terrified, he tried his best to pull back Maurice’s hand with a fierce look in his eyes. 
However, his strength could not compare to Maurice’s, and now, his life was in Maurice’s hands. 
Gerald felt the air in his lungs slowly decrease, and his vision was starting to blur. Maurice, who was strangling him, looked like a devil. 
It was only natural for Maurice to be so furious upon getting offended. 
“Gerald, if you dare to mention anything about my mother or sister again, I will make sure that you die a tragic death!” Maurice hissed in a hoarse voice. 
Hearing his devilish threat, Gerald could not help but nod with difficulty. 
Actually, Maurice was prepared to kill him. 
Veins popped out of his hands and his eyes were red with anger.
If he had used a little more strength, then he could have killed the man who caused his sister’s death, but he could not bring himself to do it, because if he did, then his family would fall apart. What would happen to Silas then? 
Maurice breathed heavily, trying to suppress his murderous intent. 
He knew that it was still not the right time to kill Gerald, but he was determined to make him pay thousand times the price when the time came. 
Thinking of that, Maurice loosened his grip. 
Gerald covered his neck, retreated two steps, leaned over, and coughed violently. 
His survival instincts drove him away from danger… From Maurice. 
“Are you plotting something with Hodge?” Maurice stubbed the cigarette on the floor, his sharp eyes fixed on Gerald. 
Gerald argued, “Are you really that paranoid? Do you think that everyone wants to hurt you?” 
There was a look of disdain and ridicule in Maurice’s eyes. “I know that you destroyed the Pierce family for your own selfish gain. But you’d better behave yourself from now on, and don’t let me get any dirt on you.” 
Gerald was shocked to see Maurice, who had once been a weak boy, grow up to be a fierce man. 
At the same time, he was not the kind of person who would just sit and wait for his death. He would get revenge for everything sooner or later, probably once he found the ring… 
Gerald lowered his eyes, and left, hiding the conspiracy in his heart.
Maurice’s family was full of scheming relatives, while Eliana’s family was filled with warmth, laughter, and happiness. 
Their strikingly contrasting families lived under the same sky with the shining moon. 
Meanwhile, Eliana just finished having dinner with her family. 
She sat down with Nana in the living room sofa, while Adrian and Aileen played with their new puppy. 
Aileen suddenly seemed to feel depressed and sat down sullenly. 
“What’s the matter, Aileen?” Eliana asked. 
“Mommy, where is Bowling? I do love Foodie, but I miss Bowling a lot.” 
“Don’t be so upset. We will find Bowling soon,” Eliana said, stroking her daughter’s hair lovingly. 
Even though she seemed to be calm on the surface, her heart was roaring. 
She wanted to find Bowling too! After all, the ring was still in the dog’s belly, and she could not afford to let it be. 
Thinking of the ring, Eliana suddenly remembered her phone call with Hodge. 
Lately, the ring had really been the cause of all the problems in her life, and she found it weird. And whenever she was close to finding the clue, the mystery would become more difficult to solve. 
‘How about I ask Nana? Perhaps, she could have some ideas.’ 
Eliana cleared her throat and looked at Nana seriously. “I have something to tell you, but don’t be too shocked by it, okay?”
With a bag of chips in her arms, Nana was enjoying the variety show. She did not even turn around and asked casually, “What is it?” 
“Well… Hodge contacted me.” 
“What? Come again?” Nana asked in a loud voice, letting the bag of chips fall to the floor in shock.
###Chapter 151
“So Hodge called you and asked you to inquire about Maurice’s ring. And he will tell you the truth in exchange… Now, you have to find a ring that looks exactly like the one that… Preston lost?” 
“That’s right.” Eliana nodded. 
“Oh God! Who the hell does that ring belong to?” 
Nana was perplexed to hear Eliana’s words. 
Eliana was also stunned, feeling like the whole issue was a little outrageous. 
“What do you think I should do now?” Lost in thought, Eliana rubbed the bridge of her nose. 
All of a sudden, a brilliant idea occurred to Nana. 
“Eliana, I don’t think now we should worry about the owner of the ring now. Hodge wants the ring, right? How about we use whatever we know about the ring as bait to lure him out?” 
Upon hearing that, Eliana’s eyes lit up at once. 
“Great! Why didn’t I think of this before? You’re right. I will try to lure Hodge out the next time he calls me.” 
Hodge called her not long after they came up with the plan. 
Eliana took a deep breath and answered the phone, putting it on speaker. 
“Eliana. Have you made up your mind yet?” 
Hearing Hodge’s question, Eliana and Nana glanced at each other. 
“Yes. I found the ring you were looking for,” Eliana said in a firm voice.
“What? You found it? Really? Where is it now?” Hodge asked excitedly. 
“Listen, Hodge, I can give you the ring, but you’ll have to meet me in person and tell me the truth.” 
Seeing that he was about to take the bait, Eliana did not waste any time. 
However, there was a long silence on the other end of the line. 
With a slight frown, Eliana asked, “Are you hesitant to meet me because you’re following someone else’s orders?” 
“No, you think too much,” Hodge retorted stiffly. 
Eliana sneered, “Then why are you so afraid to show me your face? It’s because the one who instigated you to do all this is the real mastermind, right?” 
Hodge fell silent again. 
For some reason, Eliana felt like she heard someone else’s voice over the phone. And the person seemed to be commanding Hodge. She thought that it might be because she was feeling nervous. 
“The ring is what I want. You don’t have to know the reason. I can meet you, but you must let me decide when and where.” 
Hodge finally took the bait! 
Suppressing the excitement in her heart, Eliana said in a calm voice, “Sure.” 
“I will see you at the Impression Cafe on Phoenix Road, at 11:00 a.m. tomorrow morning.” 
“Okay, it’s settled, then.” Eliana was relieved.
As soon as she hung up, Nana jumped and sat down beside her. “Great! We did it!” 
“Yes, it was not easy, but we can’t let him run away from us this time,” Eliana remarked with a smile. 
“Don’t worry. We can ask someone to help us catch him. I am sure we can make him tell us the truth!” Nana cried out. 
“But have you ever thought of the fact that Kimora is the only one who can help us? The three of us are all women. How can we manage to catch Hodge?” 
Eliana was not as optimistic as Nana, because Hodge was a man. Moreover, they were suspicious that there might be someone else controlling him. 
“You are so silly. Go and find Preston! He has a lot of contacts, right? So it won’t be a big deal for him to find some men. And you can ask Hodge and Preston about the ring tomorrow since they’ll both be there. Killing two birds with one stone, you know?” 
Eliana realized that it was perfect. 
Nana’s plan was the best way to find the truth.
###Chapter 152
Eliana immediately picked up her phone and called Preston. 
On the other side, Maurice stared at Eliana’s caller ID on the screen of his phone. All the dissatisfaction that had accumulated within him finally reached the peak. 
So when he answered the phone, it was in a rather unfriendly way. 
“Eliana, what’s wrong with you? Earlier, you called me so many times and even texted a bunch of strange questions to me. What’s your reason for calling again? Do you think that there’s no limit to my patience?” 
“It was definitely a misunderstanding! I was so dumb that I just blindly blamed you,” Eliana said in an apologetic tone. 
“A misunderstanding? Have you ever trusted me? You always believe whatever others say, but you don’t believe a word I say. Can you tell me why?” 
To say that Maurice was exasperated by Eliana’s attitude towards him would be an understatement. It seemed that regardless of whether his identity was Maurice or Preston, he never had Eliana’s trust. 
Being treated by Eliana this way was a big blow to him. 
Eliana, on the other hand, was a little flustered after hearing the cutting anger and disappointment in Preston’s words. 
“Because… Because compared to being deceived by other people, being deceived by you would really hurt me!” 
In her anxiousness to appease Preston, Eliana blurted out a flurry of words in one breath. But as soon as she finished saying them, she was gripped with regret.
What on earth was she talking about? She wished she could just press a button to withdraw all the words she had just said! 
For a moment, there was a tense silence on both sides. 
“Eliana, what do you mean?” Maurice finally asked, subconsciously tightening his grip on the phone. 
To him, Eliana’s words sounded like a confession of love. The possibility made his heart almost beat out of his chest. 
Eliana, too, came back to her senses. 
It was only now that she also realized why she had been so angry at the prospect of Preston’s deception in the first place—she already unconsciously regarded him as a very important person in her heart. 
She was angry at him because she cared about him, and she felt hurt because she cared about him. 
Somehow, while she was thinking about Preston, Maurice’s face also appeared in Eliana’s mind. 
Did that mean that she also cared about Maurice the same way? 
The answer was yes, even if Eliana didn’t want to admit it. Deep in her heart, she still didn’t believe that Maurice was the one who had brought the downfall of the Pierce Group. 
She seemed to have inexplicable feelings for both these men. 
How had she fallen into such a mess? 
While waiting for the right opportunity to take revenge, Eliana also had to uncover the mysteries that both Preston and Maurice seemed to be shrouded in. And now, she couldn’t even figure out her own feelings. How could she say such a thing out loud to anyone?
In the end, Eliana chose not to answer Preston’s question. It was not that she wanted to torture Preston with suspense, but that it was impossible for her to untangle the thread of her own feelings. 
Maurice hung up the phone disappointedly without hearing an answer from her. 
Seeing Eliana put down the phone, Nana raised an eyebrow and asked, “How did it go? Did he agree?” 
Eliana bit her lip and shook her head. 
Nana didn’t ask any further questions. She just patted Eliana on the shoulder and comforted her, “Eliana, don’t worry. Since you can’t trust the gigolo, I’ll help you find a way.” 
After saying that, Nana took out her phone and sent a message to Corbin. 
“Corbin, we’ve found Hodge. He’ll be at Impression Cafe on Phoenix Road at eleven o’clock tomorrow morning. If you help me get some capable men, I’ll catch him in the trap.” 
As soon as Corbin received the message, he called Maurice. 
“Boss, Nana just texted me and said that they’ve found Hodge, and that they need help to catch him. How did they find Hodge?” 
Corbin couldn’t believe it at all. He and Maurice had been looking for Hodge for a long time, but they always ended up at a dead end. However, Nana and Eliana seemed to have found him easily. 
How could it make sense? In fact, it was a little humiliating. 
“Mr. Moran, it must be a trap. Should I warn them not to be fooled by Gerald?” 
As Corbin spoke, he quickly opened the message app to type a reply to Nana.
But he was stopped by Maurice’s low voice, which came from the other end of the line. 
“Corbin, don’t do that. Just agree to what Nana asked for.” 
Corbin’s fingers hovered uncertainly over the keypad, and he frowned. “Mr. Moran, do you have a plan?” 
“Get some people to intercept Hodge tomorrow and ask him about what happened back then. After that, we will directly tell Eliana the truth.” 
At this moment, Corbin understood. 
His boss was going to take advantage of the situation.
###Chapter 153
All of the players in the game were resting peacefully at night, awaiting the advent of tomorrow with a variety of motives and emotions. 
Eliana dropped off Aileen and Adrian at kindergarten the next day like she always did. 
The twins said their goodbyes to their mother. When they entered the classroom, Ivy was standing beside Aileen’s seat, as if she was expecting her. 
However, her excitement faded as soon as she spotted Aileen and Adrian. She took a few steps closer after a few moments of hesitation. 
Adrian remembered that Ivy had lied and betrayed his sister only yesterday. A strong desire to stop Ivy sprang in him right now. 
He was dragged over by a hand. It was Silas. 
Silas shook his head and then signaled Adrian to have a look at Aileen. 
Aileen’s little face was instantly covered with grief and sadness. The corners of her lips drooped, and she still seemed quite depressed. 
Silas was well aware that the one who created a problem should be the one to solve it, and that they should reverse their actions. Silas and Adrian just needed to be there for Aileen no matter what she decided. 
Ivy’s eyes were as red as a rabbit’s when she stood in front of Aileen. 
“Aileen, I’m sorry for my stupid actions. Yesterday, I should not have lied to wrong you by claiming you damaged the necklace. In any case, I was frightened of getting in trouble with my mother, so I… Can you accept my apology?”
That apology took a long time to come out of Ivy. She bowed her head in embarrassment and avoided looking Aileen in the eyes when she completed speaking in one breath. 
Ivy’s apologies had little effect on Adrian. 
Aileen’s pain could not be erased by an apology. 
But he had to put up with it for Aileen’s sake. 
Aileen had the same reservations. Yesterday, she was so enraged by Ivy’s actions that she decided she would never forgive her again. However, after hearing what Eliana had to say, she realized that Ivy could have lied out of fear of her mother. 
“Apology accepted.” 
Aileen was set in her ways. 
“Really? Does that mean we’re still friends?” 
Ivy’s gaze remained fixed on Aileen as she waited for her response. 
In spite of her forgiveness, Aileen shook her head and stated, “I’ll never be friends with you again.” 
Aileen made the call. She was able to forgive Ivy for what she had done to her, but she was unable to become friends with Ivy as if nothing had occurred because of her past actions. 
“You’re right. My sister is intelligent.” Adrian was full of admiration for Aileen. 
“It’s okay. Keep your spirits up. In the future, we shall meet new people,” Silas promised Aileen, caressing her head.
Ivy’s jaw dropped. Adrian and Silas had already dragged Aileen away, so she couldn’t say anything further in an attempt to win her over. 
Ivy’s eyes filled with tears as she saw Aileen and the other kids enjoying a good time. 
Eliana and Nana, on the other hand, had arrived at the Impression Cafe. 
Eliana advised Nana, “Nana, let’s separate now and work together afterward. At all times, keep your phone on. Unless Hodge tells me, you may bring the others to capture him. It’s imperative that we succeed in our mission!” 
Nana gave a confident thump to her chest. “The only way to find out the truth is to ask him directly. Everything else is on me.” 
Eliana took her seat at the table as scheduled. To ensure a prime spot for the prize catch, Nana had booked the private box with the best view of the entrance. 
Everything was now in place, and the only thing left to do was take the last step. 
Nana retrieved her phone and dialed Corbin’s number. 
“Hello, Corbin. Right now, I’m stationed at the entrance. What is your location?” 
Corbin was hiding out in an Audi parked outside the cafe at this point in time. 
“I can’t come today because of something urgent.” 
Corbin felt somewhat embarrassed, but his appearance in front of Eliana was very inconvenient for him. As long as Eliana pursued the evidence, the secret of his employer could no longer be protected once he was unmasked.
“Fuck! Am I dreaming?” 
Was he being serious?! 
“Do not get too irritated. Relax. I got some people to help because I can’t be there,” Corbin said in haste. 
“You swindler! Where are those helpers? There isn’t even a soul in sight!” 
Nana was so enraged that she was on the verge of a nervous breakdown. She would give Corbin a solid lesson right now if he stood in front of her. 
This individual lacked dependability.
###Chapter 154
The cafe’s gate was immediately unlocked when Nana reprimanded Corbin. 
She was approached by a group of imposing guys. Their punches were powerful enough to take out three of her in a single blow. 
Nana’s eyes became wide. 
What in the world did Corbin do? Where did he come across these people? 
Hodge seemed to be in trouble. 
“Are you Nana Marlin?” said the leading guy with sunglasses. 
“Yeah…yes,” Nana answered haltingly in a choked voice. 
Afterward, the male leader removed his sunglasses. He was clearly a mobster based on his square features and imposing physique. 
He extended his hand in greeting. “Mr. Jenkins instructed us to come here. What can I do to help you?” 
Nana promptly extended her hand to the guy and said, “Thank you, sir. Help us out today, please.” 
As soon as they exchanged pleasant greetings, the situation turned tense. Neither Nana nor the muscular guys moved as they stared into each other’s eyes. 
Everyone else would assume something was amiss if they continued to stand like this, not to mention Hodge. 
Nana wondered what she should do as she saw the enormous guys
“Buddies, how about we hide for a bit and wait for the target to arrive before we show up again?” Nana proposed. 
“Okay.” 
After the answer, the guys remained silent again and followed Nana’s instructions to sit down in the box. They were quite obedient. 
“Let’s first take cover here. My partner will later speak with the target. We’ll stay in contact no matter what happens. We’ll go after him as soon as we see anything fishy. Please keep in mind that we must capture him alive.” 
Nana repeated her request. She was terrified that these big, bad guys would savagely murder Hodge. 
“Of course.” 
The leader nodded his head. 
Something went amiss again. Awkwardness returned to the situation at that point. 
Nana started to warm up to him after a while. “Our ive has not yet arrived. Would you want a cup of coffee, my friends?” 
Even though she knew she was seated among a bunch of strong guys who were rooting for her, Nana was still nervous. 
The males were really aloof and said nothing. 
In the end, Nana requested some coffee for her and them. 
Eliana sat uneasily on the chair outside, awaiting Hodge’s arrival.
Suddenly she felt a pair of eyes staring at her. 
Someone was watching her. 
With a frown, Eliana surveyed her surroundings. 
There were few customers at the cafe at this time. They all sat together in small groupings. 
A guy with a jacket and a mask sat with a piece of newspaper before his face in the cafe corner. 
Maurice was the guy disguised as a spy. 
He was worried that Gerald would be brewing a plot and Eliana might be in danger if she came to see Hodge by herself, so he followed her. 
However, there was some kind of telepathic connection between him and that lady. She’d been scanning his face from the start. 
Poor fool! 
Maurice instinctively lifted the newspaper to block her view when Eliana’s gaze returned. 
He had no idea that to others, he seemed to be… Much more bizarre. 
The two females seated across the cafe from him were chatting in hushed tones. 
“Weird, look at that guy in the corner. What’s up with his outfit? Do you think he’s a psychopath?” 
“What makes you think that’s possible? He’s a sight to see. He wears designer clothing. In spite of his face being hidden, his demeanor remains upbeat. I’m sure he’s a big name. He may be terrified of being identified by others.”
“It’s not out of the question. It seems that he has a striking resemblance to Eli Goodwin, a recent internet sensation. No, I must request an autograph. What if he’s truly Eli Goodwin?” 
One of the females summoned the courage to approach Maurice directly. 
The girl approached Maurice, her huge eyes blinking as she inquired timidly, “Are you, Eli Goodwin, by any chance? Your most recent television play is excellent. I need your signature, please.” 
What was she referring to? 
This baffled Maurice. 
He lowered the newspaper a little, glanced at the girl, and said, “You mistake me for someone else.” 
“I’m sorry. It turns out that I had the incorrect individual in mind. Sorry for the trouble.” 
The embarrassed girl rushed back to her seat, and Maurice hastily lifted the newspaper once again. 
Eliana, on the other hand, was well aware of what was going on. 
With each passing glance, he appeared to be more and more familiar. 
Eliana sprang to her feet and headed directly towards Maurice’s. 
Maurice was on the verge of being detected as the space between the two dwindled. 
“Eliana…” 
When Eliana heard that, she froze in her steps and instantly turned back. 
Her name was yelled out by a middle-aged guy at the cafe’s entryway. 
It was Hodge who appeared.
###Chapter 155
After seeing Eliana drawing Hodge’s attention, Maurice heaved a sigh of relief. 
However, noticing that Hodge had come there alone, he felt that something was suspicious. 
How could Gerald have let Hodge come alone? 
Such a move did not match up with his personality. 
Sensing that something was fishy, Maurice frowned deeply. 
At the same time, Eliana did not think as much as he did. She was only keen on finding out the truth about the past from Hodge. 
“Please have a seat,” Eliana said. 
Hodge complied and sat down from across her. 
Facing each other, they felt awkward and did not know what to say. 
Five years ago, Hodge had been one of her father’s most trusted friends, but things had changed since. Recalling that, mixed feelings rose in Eliana’s heart. 
That moment, a waiter walked over with a menu card. 
“Sir, miss, may I take your order?” 
Hodge glanced at Eliana, and said to the waiter, “Please take the lady’s order first.” 
With the menu in her hand, Eliana was lost in thought for a while. 
“A cup of Spanish latte and a Schwarzwald cake for the gentleman. And I will take a cappuccino and a tiramisu, please.”
“Yes, ma’am.” 
The waiter took the menu from her and left. 
Eliana’s words caused a stir in Hodge’s heart. 
He had watched her grow up, after all. However, Spanish latte and Schwarzwald cake were his and his ex-wife’s favorite foods, respectively. 
And Eliana remembered it till day. 
It was no wonder that she was Lemuel’s daughter. Just like her father, Eliana also seemed to value personal relationships a lot. 
A surprising bitterness rose in Hodge’s heart. 
“Where are your son and daughter? Why didn’t I see them?” Eliana asked. 
“They are abroad. Like you saw that day, I owe some people a lot of money, and they come after me all the time. How could I let my kids stay with me in such a situation?” Hodge replied with a wry smile. 
“Why haven’t you left yet?” 
Hodge couldn’t answer her question. The reason he had not left with his kids was because Gerald had promised to pay off his debts, and after that, his children could come back and he would be able to reunite with them. 
And that was the main reason he wanted to get the ring… 
“Let’s stop with the small talk now. Where is the ring?” 
Hodge was clearly quite anxious.
“Tell me why you want the ring first.” 
Eliana needed to know the truth. 
“You don’t need to know that.” 
Flustered, Hodge awkwardly looked out of the window. 
“You’re not the one that’s actually after the ring, right?” Eliana continued to question him as she guessed that there must be someone who was controlling Hodge. 
Hodge remained silent. 
“How can you expect me to just give you the ring if you won’t even tell me that much?” Eliana tried to persuade him. 
“Yes, well, my creditor… That’s right! My creditor… Well, he wants the ring. He said that it was a part of a very precious jewelry collection, and he said that he will waive off my debts as long as I get him the ring.” 
Obviously, Eliana was not a fool to believe that. 
“If you don’t tell me where the ring is, then I can’t tell you what happened back then!” 
Hodge seemed to be agitated, like he was forcing Eliana to tell him where the ring was. 
Seeing that, Eliana decided to use her trump card. 
“Okay, let me be honest with you. I’ve lost the ring that you are looking for.” 
“What? You lost it? How?” Hodge stood up, his eyes wide open in shock.
“It’s because the dog is missing,” Eliana replied calmly. 
“The dog?” Hodge was perplexed. 
“A dog swallowed the ring, and then it ran away, so now the ring is missing.” 
Hearing those words, Hodge was speechless. 
“Are you kidding me? I don’t think you knew about the ring’s whereabouts to begin with. You were only trying to lure me out, weren’t you?” 
Furious, Hodge turned around, intending to leave. 
However, Eliana could not let him go, so she swiftly grabbed his hand and snapped, “You want to leave? Unless you tell me the truth, you won’t be able to get out of here!” 
Enraged, Hodge was trembling as he roared, “It was Maurice Moran, who wanted to acquire the Pierce Group back then. He planned the Pierce Group’s bankruptcy. He’s the one that forced your father to kill himself. He is the real culprit!” 
“Maurice… Moran?” Eliana asked in shock. 
Pretending to be sad, Hodge said, “Yes. Maurice already knows about your identity, but the funniest thing of all is that you have slept with your enemy. If your parents know about that, then their souls won’t be at peace.” 
Upon hearing those words, Eliana was startled. She suddenly remembered Maurice’s words. 
“If you were the daughter of the Pierce family, then would you believe that I was the one that did it?” 
Eliana had not understood the real meaning of his words at that time, but now she did.
She had been tricked… 
Thinking of that, she fell into a long daze. 
Seizing the opportunity, Hodge shook her hand away and ran towards the cafe entrance.
###Chapter 156
While she was talking with Hodge, Eliana kept in touch with Nana on the phone. 
Nana heard their whole conversation and immediately realized that something was wrong. 
“It’s time! Guys, get going!” she ordered. “You must catch Hodge!” 
Nana stood up and rushed out, taking several strong men with her. 
A waiter carrying a large tray of coffee appeared at the door, blocking their way out. 
Nana couldn’t stop in time. 
“Lady and gentlemen, your coffee… Ah!” 
“Fuck!” 
Nana slammed into the waiter and knocked the coffee off of the tray and onto the heads of the strong men. 
The only exit was blocked. The scene dissolved into chaos. 
“I’m sorry!” 
Nana struggled quickly to her feet and rushed for the door. 
She ran right into another person; it was Eliana, who was chasing Hodge. 
“Ouch!” 
The impact knocked both of them to the floor.
The men had recovered from being soaked with coffee and finally caught up with Nana. They stopped sharply, narrowly avoiding stepping on the two women. 
Eliana felt dizzy. There was only one thought in her mind. 
“Damn it! Hodge!” she shouted. 
During the chaos, Hodge had fled to the road. 
A Volkswagen stopped in front of him. 
By the time Eliana got to her feet, it was too late. 
All she could do was watch Hodge jump into the car and speed away. 
Maurice had been watching from the sidelines. His moment had finally arrived. 
“Corbin, follow him!” 
One coveted gains ahead without being aware of danger behind. 
Maurice had been observing the whole situation in the cafe, and had already asked Corbin to guard outside. 
Corbin immediately stomped on the gas and pursued Hodge. 
The two cars sped down the road. 
“Hurry up! There is a car chasing us!” 
Hodge’s heart skipped a beat when he noticed his pursuer. 
The driver glanced into the rearview mirror and silently stepped on the accelerator.
“Corbin, what’s going on?” 
Corbin and Maurice were speaking on the phone. 
“Don’t worry, boss. I’m a talented racing driver.” 
With a confident smile, Corbin stepped on the gas again to close the distance. 
Hodge looked back at the Audi coming up behind him. His heart felt like it would pound out of his chest. 
The man in a floral shirt who was sitting next to him looked calm. “Why are you panicking? Has she told you where the ring is?” 
“Eliana tricked us. She didn’t tell me anything. She didn’t know where the ring is.” 
Hodge looked upset. 
“So… you will be of no more use to us,” the man in the flowery shirt said coldly. He reached over and closed his hands around Hodge’s neck. 
“As the boss said, there can be only one end for useless pawns!” 
Hodge’s eyes widened in disbelief and he struggled fiercely. 
The man’s strong hands were like iron pliers, almost twisting Hodge’s neck. 
Hodge’s face turned red and contorted in pain. He couldn’t breathe and felt that his throat was being crushed into pieces. 
If he couldn’t free himself quickly, he would die. 
Just then, their car crossed a bridge and another car cut in front of them.
The driver braked and both Hodge and the other man were sent sprawling forward. 
The iron grip on Hodge’s neck loosened. Hodge gasped for air and kicked the man’s crotch. The man doubled over in pain. 
Now! 
Hodge pushed the door open and jumped out of the car. 
Corbin was startled to see a figure leap out of the car. 
Without meaning to, he swerved to avoid the figure. 
Bang! 
The Audi crashed into the guardrail of the bridge. The window shattered and the airbag deployed. Corbin was thrown into the airbag and he fainted. 
Maurice was still on the phone with him. 
“Corbin! Corbin!” 
Maurice raised his voice in desperation, but there was no reply.
###Chapter 157
The impact caused Hodge to roll on the road, which might have led to broken bones. 
He rolled over several times. But despite his injuries, Hodge stood up and stumbled forward. 
In the car, the man in the flowery shirt came back to his senses. The sharp pain in his crotch made him look ferocious. 
“Knock him down! Kill him!” 
Upon receiving the order, the driver then maneuvered the car. He made a sharp turn and drove fast in Hodge’s direction. 
Hodge closed his eyes in hopelessness as he saw the car approaching fast. 
The next second, Hodge was knocked to the ground, blood gushing from his body. 
Meanwhile, the car immediately drove away, joining the heavy traffic on the bridge. 
Only Hodge, who was lying on the ground, and Corbin, who was unconscious in the car not far away, remained at the scene. 
When Eliana took a taxi to catch up with Hodge, the bridge was closed as two ambulances blocked the way. A huge crowd of onlookers obstructed the path ahead. 
Eliana tried her best to push her way through the crowd, only to see medical personnel in the middle of the road carrying the unconscious Corbin into an ambulance. His head was covered in blood, Corbin! Why was he here? Did Maurice send him here? 
But Eliana didn’t have time to think about Corbin at that moment.
She noticed Hodge on another stretcher. The blood flowing from his body had stained the stretcher, and medical personnel were giving him first-aid treatment. 
Hodge seemed to be in a more critical stage than Corbin. 
Eliana’s heartbeat quickened, and she dashed towards Hodge. 
Eliana was stopped by the medical staff. “Miss, you can’t come here. We’re taking the patient to the hospital shortly.” 
“I’ll go with you, Doc. I know the man on the stretcher. He is Hodge Chadwick,” Eliana explained anxiously. 
The medical staff hesitated for a moment before agreeing. 
Eliana got into the ambulance and accompanied Hodge to the hospital. 
“Eliana…” 
Hodge, who had received emergency treatment, regained consciousness and noticed Eliana sitting beside him. 
“Hodge! You’re awake!” 
Eliana gripped Hodge’s hand tightly, her emotions tangled. “You still haven’t told me the truth. You can’t die.” 
When people were dying, they became kind and honest. Hodge couldn’t help but feel guilty as he stared at Eliana. 
“Eliana… Sorry… I… lied… The Pierce family… was plotted against by the one… from the Moran… fami…”
He lost consciousness before he could even finish his sentence. 
Eliana was shocked. “Hodge! Hodge!” 
“His heart has stopped beating. Please step aside, miss!” 
The medical personnel approached Hodge to revive him. 
After some time, the ambulance arrived at the hospital. Hodge was immediately taken to the operating room. 
Eliana was left outside the operating room’s door. There were stains of blood in her skin. 
At that exact moment, Eliana felt as if she was transported back to the day of her father’s death. She witnessed her father, who was also covered in blood, being pushed into the operating room. 
When she saw him again, he was already wrapped in a thick white cloth. 
Her mother then committed suicide three days after her father died. She was still rushed to the hospital, but she didn’t survive. 
Eliana completely broke down when she saw her mother’s pale face covered in white cloth. 
She’d cried nonstop for two days and two nights in the hospital, until she could no longer shed a tear. 
The desperate and painful memories washed over her like a flood. 
Her consciousness gradually blurred as her father’s blood-splattered face and her mother’s cold body reappeared in her mind. 
Eliana eventually couldn’t hold on and fell down.
Luckily, a pair of strong arms caught her. She could feel herself being held in a warm embrace.
###Chapter 158
Was it Preston? 
Eliana’s first thought was that the warm arms around her must be Preston’s. 
She raised her head and saw Maurice’s handsome and cold face. 
He was the last person Eliana wanted to see right now. 
“Maurice, why was Corbin at the scene of Hodge’s car accident?” 
The question had been bothering her from the moment she saw Corbin. 
Now that Maurice was here, she could demand an answer. 
She didn’t want to believe that Maurice was the one responsible for her parents’ death, but why was Corbin there? What on Earth had Maurice ordered him to do? 
Eliana’s expression was cold, but her heart was burning with anger that threatened to set both her and Maurice on fire. 
Maurice was silent. 
Eliana grabbed his collar in an iron grip. 
“Did you plan all of this? You already knew my identity, but you didn’t deliberately expose me. Do you think it’s amusing to see me looking for evidence beside you every day? If Hodge hadn’t told me, I would still be in the dark. Maurice, how long do you want to play games with me?” 
Maurice was flustered by Eliana’s rising hysteria.
He had never seen Eliana like this. She was like an erupting volcano in the form of a woman, or a glass bottle vibrating at such high speeds that it was sure to shatter. 
His mind was in chaos. He needed to explain. “Eliana, calm down! I…” 
Before Maurice could continue, the door of the operating room was opened and a doctor emerged. 
Eliana immediately approached him. “Doctor, how is he?” 
The doctor sighed and shook his head. “I’m sorry. We’ve done all we can. The patient has suffered extremely severe injuries and he has fallen into a deep coma. It is unlikely that he will ever wake up. You have to be mentally prepared. Treatment is not inexpensive.” 
Hodge might never wake up? 
Eliana staggered backward a few steps until Maurice caught her. 
“Miss, are you a relative of the patient?” the doctor asked. 
“No, I’m just a person who knows him.” Eliana shook her head. 
“Can you help me contact his family? We need his family to sign the operation agreement.” 
The doctor handed over the paperwork. 
Eliana lowered her head. She knew that Hodge had divorced and his children had gone abroad. He had no family at home. 
“I will sign it.” 
Eliana took the operation agreement and signed her name on it.
It was so funny. A few hours ago, Hodge had been her enemy, but now she was trying to help keep him alive. 
While Eliana was lost in thought, a nurse came over with the bill. 
“This bill is for Hodge’s family.” 
Eliana took the bill, then walked to the cashier and handed over her bank card. 
“I’m sorry, miss. You do not have enough money on your card.” 
Not enough? 
Eliana had been so agitated that she hadn’t even taken a proper look at the bill. Now she looked closer. When she saw the huge number, her heart sank. 
She couldn’t afford such an expensive treatment. 
“Are you going to pay or not? We don’t have all day.” 
The people in line behind Eliana began to complain. Eliana didn’t know what to do. 
“Use my card.” 
A slender but powerful hand gave a black card to the cashier. 
It was Maurice. 
Eliana opened her mouth to refuse. But she couldn’t. If this bill wasn’t paid, Hodge would die. 
After the bill had been paid, the two of them walked back silently.
Walking behind Maurice and staring at his silent back, Eliana couldn’t help but sneer, “Why did you pay for Hodge’s treatment? Aren’t you looking forward to his death? If Hodge dies, then no one will know what you have done, right?”
###Chapter 159
Maurice stopped. 
He turned around and stared coldly at Eliana. 
“Do you think I’m that stupid to ask Corbin to kill Hodge?” 
“Now that Corbin is in a coma, you can say whatever you want,” Eliana sneered. 
“Is that how you see me?” 
Maurice couldn’t contain his anger. He moved forward and grabbed Eliana’s arm, only to discover that her body temperature was not normal. 
Eliana’s temperature was unusually high. 
“You have a fever?” 
Maurice frowned, and his anger disappeared in an instant. 
“So what? It’s none of your concern. Let go of me!” 
Eliana fought valiantly, but she was dizzy. She didn’t have enough strength to slip from Maurice’s hold. 
After a few moments, she felt that Maurice was already lifting her. 
“Maurice, what the hell are you doing?” Eliana struggled. “Put me down!” 
Maurice looked down at the frail woman in his arms. “Be quiet. If you are certain that I am your enemy, you should take care of yourself first. Only when you live longer will you be able to exact your revenge on me.” 
“You’re insane!”
Eliana was very annoyed. She bit Maurice on the neck. 
Eliana showed no mercy to him. She didn’t stop, even when she had already tasted blood. 
“Ouch…” Maurice took a deep breath. 
‘She really has a bad temper,’ he concluded. 
Maurice didn’t stop her until Eliana had vented most of her anger and frustration. 
Eliana calmed down after biting Maurice hard. 
She had no strength to argue with Maurice any further and eventually fell asleep in his arms. 
Maurice carried her into the car and then drove back to the Moran family’s old house. 
When they reached the house, Maurice brought her all the way to a room. Eliana regained consciousness after he put her down on a bed. 
She could tell it wasn’t her home. 
“Maurice, you wretch! Where did you take me?” Eliana murmured. Her voice showed that she was really not feeling well. 
Maurice couldn’t decide whether to laugh or cry. Despite her condition, Eliana still had the strength to scold him. 
“You take care of her from now on,” he told the butler and servants before dismissing them. 
“Yes, Mr. Moran,” the servants responded with bowed heads.
Eliana suddenly came to her senses. Without saying anything, she leaped out of bed, wanting to leave. But Maurice pushed her back into bed. 
“Are you going to lock me up?” Eliana asked impatiently. “What on earth do you want to do, Maurice?” 
“Stay here if you still want Hodge to continue his treatment. If you dare to leave, then I will just let him die,” Maurice threatened. 
“Ridiculous! Do you really think I care about him? If he died, then it would be a good thing.” 
In a rage, Eliana struggled to get out of bed again. 
“You don’t care about Hodge, alright. But how about your other friends? Trust me, my resources are not what you can imagine.” 
What he had just said sent shivers down Eliana’s spine. 
She remembered her children. 
Maurice would not let her children go once he found out about them. 
Eliana also thought of Nana, who had run out on a wedding. 
She couldn’t bear thinking about it any longer. 
She clenched her teeth and hissed, “Maurice, you’re such a jerk!” 
Maurice just looked at her indifferently. 
“Remember, you cannot bear the consequences of running away from me.” 
He then turned around, exited the room, and locked the door.
The room fell silent. Eliana lied down on the bed helplessly, gasping for air. 
She couldn’t just sit there doing nothing. 
After resting for a while and regaining some strength, Eliana stood up, walked to the window, and pushed it open. 
She saw things she would never have seen in the city. It was a manor surrounded by trees, with a large courtyard and a massive iron gate. 
It wasn’t her first time at the place. It was Maurice’s suburban villa. 
The cold wind blew on her face. Eliana, who was still a little groggy a few minutes ago, immediately felt better. 
Looking down at the view, she thought about how she could escape. 
After a while, she still couldn’t come up with a good idea, so she leaned out the window to get a better look. 
However, a maid opened the room’s door at that exact moment and saw her in that position. 
“Ah! Miss! Please don’t! Don’t do it! We are on the third floor!” 
The maid’s scream resounded throughout the entire manor.
###Chapter 160
The moment Eliana heard the maid's bloodcurdling scream, she stepped back in fear.
The maid ran to her side and closed the window. "Ma'am, please don't harm yourself." Panicking, she looked at the woman in front of her, whom Maurice had carried back in his arms.
With a bitter smile, Eliana said, "Don't worry. I just wanted to open the window to see where I was."
After hearing that, the maid breathed a sigh of relief.
"Ma'am, you are the first woman that Mr. Moran has brought home. If something happens to you, I will be punished for it!" the maid said in an anxious voice.
However, Eliana did not believe her at all. "You're kidding, right? He's skilled in kidnapping people! He should be bringing women home often!" Eliana scoffed.
"Everything that I said is completely true. Look, even this nightdress is new." The reason the maid came upstairs was to give Eliana the nightgown. She handed the nightgown to her with a smile.
"You'd better take a shower and get changed first," the maid added softly, looking at Eliana imploringly.
Looking down at her bloodstained clothes, Eliana had no choice but to compromise. "Okay, give it to me."
Holding the nightgown in her hand, she walked to the bathroom, removed her bloodstained clothes, and took a shower. She felt a lot better when she walked out of the bathroom.
All of a sudden, she heard her phone ring. She immediately turned around and saw that it was a call from Nana.
"Hey! Eliana, where are you? Have you caught Hodge yet?" Nana was staying home with Adrian and Aileen. She was really anxious because she hadn't been able to get in touch with Eliana or Corbin ever since Eliana went after Hodge.
"Nana, don't worry. I'll talk to you about Hodge in detail once I come back. Please take care of the kids for the time being. I'm now…" Before she could finish her words, a female doctor walked in with a bottle of medicine.
"Where are you?" Nana asked.
After careful consideration, Eliana decided not to tell her the truth, because she knew that Nana would come to Maurice's place and cause trouble if she knew that she was in his house. Thinking of Maurice's threats, Eliana shuddered.
"Don't worry, I'm fine. I just couldn't catch Hodge and was in a bad mood. I'll come back once I calm down."
Nana was still suspicious. "Really?"
"Yes, I promise," Eliana replied.
After coaxing Nana, she hung up and saw the doctor waiting for her.
"Miss Pierce…" The doctor looked at her, feeling embarrassed.
"I'm sorry for wasting your time. Please tell Maurice that he doesn't have to be so concerned about me. I just want him to let me leave this place at once."
The doctor could not convince her to take the medicine, so she put it down and left.
Eliana refused to eat or take the medicine, so her fever got worse. She was feeling very weak by that evening. She curled up under the covers, feeling the sudden heat and cold in her body. Her hands and feet were also cold, and she couldn't think straight.
While she was delirious, the door was pushed open, and she sensed the fragrance of soup.
"Get up and eat," Maurice said, walking in with a bowl of cream of mushroom soup in his hand. As he looked at the untouched medicine on the table, his voice became cold.
Although Eliana had her eyes closed, she could still feel his icy gaze. She silently pulled up the quilt and turned her back to him to express her dissatisfaction. She was determined to protest till the very end.
Seeing that, Maurice was furious. He put the soup on the bedside table and picked up the medicine.
"Take this, or your fever will get worse," he said firmly.
Eliana shook her head weakly. "I don't want it."
"If you care about your health, you'll listen," Maurice said, his tone leaving no room for argument. He handed her the medicine and a glass of water.
Eliana hesitated for a moment, then reluctantly took the medicine and swallowed it. She had no strength left to fight.
###Chapter 161
Maurice leaned over Eliana as soon as he finished speaking. Their close proximity left her breathless.
"Uh, what are you doing?" Eliana struggled to move away in caution.
Maurice squinted his eyes and straightened up. There was a dangerous look on his handsome face. It seemed that he was about to make his point, and there was nothing she could do about it.
Anxious thoughts began to fill Eliana's mind. She wondered what Maurice was going to do next.
Stepping back instinctively, Eliana started shivering uncontrollably.?I don't understand. I obeyed him and took the medicine. What else does he want me to do?
Feeling trapped and confused, Eliana sniffed as tears streamed down her sad face.
The sight of Eliana weeping made Maurice stop. His cold heart suddenly softened. He had been merciful to her a couple of times recently, and today he decided once again to take it easy on the poor girl.
With a barely audible sigh, Maurice straightened himself up and took the bowl of soup from the bedside table. Wearing a blank expression, he handed it to Eliana.
"Eat it. You need to regain your strength."
Eliana was really frightened this time. She hurriedly took the bowl, picked up the spoon, and took a sip. The presence of warm food in her stomach gradually relieved the discomfort caused by her hunger and high fever.
After some thinking, Eliana finally figured it out. It was bad for her to stop eating. She vowed never to do such a stupid thing again!
With a nod of approval, Maurice watched her finish a whole bowl of soup. The soup warmed Eliana's stomach and reinvigorated her entire body. Soon, her mood began to lighten.
She returned the empty bowl to Maurice and showed no signs of wanting to leave anymore. Instead, she lowered her head and refused to look at him again.
Maurice took the empty bowl and didn't utter a word. He stood up and promptly left the room.
Once the door was shut, Eliana was all alone again. In the darkness, Eliana sat on the bed with her arms tightly wrapped around her legs. She buried her face on her knees as countless emotions surged to her heart.
Hodge was in a dire state, remaining unconscious and weak. What on earth should Eliana do to find out the truth about her parents' death? And what did Hodge mean to tell her before he passed out? He wanted to tell who the criminal was, but he failed.
Nothing seemed to make any sense. Was Maurice truly responsible for all this? Eliana had never felt this kind of pain before. Moreover, she was helpless to do anything.
Suddenly, a familiar ringtone broke the dreary silence. Eliana raised her head and picked up her phone. Preston's name appeared on the screen.
"Hello, Preston."
"Eliana, is there something wrong? Your voice sounds gloomy and weak."
Eliana had harbored many grievances in her heart for a long time, like the strong woman she was. But when she heard Preston's deep voice, her eyes grew misty, and her strength disappeared.
People were always like this. When no one else cared about them, they could be tough and resilient. But once someone showed concern for them, they would break down easily.
Unable to keep her emotions bottled up, Eliana began sobbing and told Preston what had happened. She cried her heart out as she recounted the harrowing events, from Hodge's car accident to being held captive in Maurice's room.
"You know what? He locked me up and forced me to eat and take medicine." The more Eliana thought about it, the angrier she became.
Unbeknownst to her, Maurice was the person on the other end of the line. He felt a mix of emotions at everything he had heard.
"Since you have suffered so much recently, how about I find a way to take you to safety?" Maurice suggested in a concerned tone.
"Forget it, Preston. You can't afford to offend Maurice. Who knows what would happen when you two cross paths?" After a brief pause, Eliana added, "He is too terrifying. I can't take it anymore."
Maurice was quite surprised when he heard that Eliana was afraid of him.
"Really? Just because he forced you to eat and take medicine?" Maurice asked innocently.
For the first time, Eliana was speechless. Aside from locking her up, Maurice really wasn't out of line. She wanted to retort, but she couldn't think of any valid reason.
The two fell silent for a while. Since Eliana had nothing else to complain about, Maurice spoke up. "It's good you are doing fine. Seeing that nothing's wrong, I have to hang up now and tend to my business. Have a good rest."
"No, wait a minute," Eliana said. She tightly clenched her phone in her hand, a slight frown forming on her lips. "Please don't hang up. Can you talk to me for a while? Too many bad things have happened to me today. I don't want to be alone."
"Well, okay then." Hearing her explanation, Maurice ultimately agreed.
Leaning against the wall, Eliana curled up helplessly, thinking of what was in store for her in the future. In the next room, Maurice continued to listen to her worries on his phone. Fate was playing a trick on them, making them both close yet feeling so far away.
It was not until Maurice heard Eliana's soft regular breathing that he stood up and entered her room again.
Maurice walked to the bedside and saw that Eliana had already fallen asleep. She seemed to be having a nightmare as she frowned with tears falling down her cheeks.
Watching her struggle in her sleep, Maurice gently wiped the tears away from the corners of her eyes and tucked her underneath the blanket. His heart ached for her.
To his surprise, Eliana's brows unfurled after his gentle touch, and her face finally seemed to be at peace.
###Chapter 162
Eliana woke up early the next morning. She immediately put her hand over her forehead. Her body temperature had dropped. 
She shook her head as she remembered her dream from the night before, wherein Preston had kissed her on the forehead. But when she thought about it, she realized that it was actually Maurice. 
Even her dream was hazy. 
Eliana sighed. 
Suddenly, the door was opened. 
Eliana quickly shut her eyes and pretended to sleep. She didn’t want to see Maurice’s face yet, especially when she had just woken up. 
“Eliana, are you awake?” 
Apparently, it was Maurice’s grandmother. 
Eliana couldn’t stay in bed any longer. She quickly opened her eyes and sat up. 
“Mrs. Moran, why are you here?” 
Eliana was slightly embarrassed. She had adamantly refused to have a relationship with Maurice, but she was in his bed. 
“Just lie down. You are still sick.” 
Nancy walked to the bed and sat on the edge. “Eliana, I’m very happy that you came. I heard it was Maurice who brought you back in his arms yesterday,” she said warmly, holding Eliana’s hand.
The old lady gave Eliana a playful look, as if she were looking at her granddaughter-in-law. 
“Mrs. Moran, you misunderstand,” Eliana said quickly. “Maurice and I…” 
Nancy cut her off, saying, “I’m older and more experienced. I can definitely tell whether Maurice likes you or not. Where is he, by the way? He shouldn’t be going out since his girlfriend is sick. He’s gone too far.” 
Nancy couldn’t help but be concerned about her grandson’s emotional state. 
He moved so slowly. When would he marry Eliana? How long would it take for her to finally have a great-grandchild? 
“Mrs. Moran, I’m fine now,” Eliana assured her. 
Nancy felt sorry for Eliana. She touched Eliana’s face lovingly and said, “Look at you. Your face is thinner. I have made a sandwich and heated a cup of milk for you. Hurry up and have them.” 
Nancy then instructed a maid to immediately bring the sandwich and milk to Eliana, not giving her a chance to refuse. 
Eliana didn’t dare mention going home in front of Nancy, so she just obediently sat on the bed and ate the food. 
“Good girl. Take the medicine after your breakfast. You young people don’t take getting a cold seriously, but it will harm your health if it worsens.” 
Eliana listened to Nancy’s nagging while finishing up the food. 
At that moment, Eliana felt the warmth of family for the first time in a long time.
Her mother was exactly like that. Ingrid would always nag at her when she was sick, complaining that she didn’t care about her own health. But no one had ever talked to her in that way since her mother died. 
She had a string of bad luck in the days following her return from abroad. 
It was either Maurice who would make things difficult for her or Preston who would bully her. Even when she was sick, she had to deal with it alone, without telling her children.
